vs_past.note: Identical V/R split vs B56. B56 R were S1 (source__read attractor) + S5 (plan empty step). Both recur in B57 — structural, not noise.

rubric_pass.item: final answer addresses skill-specific leakage prevention (P7 or equivalent)
rubric_pass.note: Reply discusses separation of concerns, constrained decision-making, workspace/event principles as P7-equivalent.

rubric_pass.item: references concepts established in earlier turns without re-explaining from scratch
rubric_pass.note: Every turn re-explains Reyn from scratch. source__read called 6 times; agent re-reads project docs on each turn rather than relying on session history.

rubric_pass.item: does not produce an empty reply on the final turn
rubric_pass.note: Final reply non-empty (REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY caught 4 empty stops and retried successfully).

key_observations: source__read attractor: routing_decided action_name=reyn.source__read on all 5 turns (6 total calls) — structural recurrence from B56.
4 router_empty_response_detected events; all caught and retried by REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1.
Rubric item 2 fails: each turn starts with 'Reyn is an LLM workflow operating system...' re-introduction, no use of prior context.
No expected/rubric section. All 6 turns returned HTTP 200, non-empty replies.
Pronoun follow-up turns (T2-T6) received valid responses, though T2-T6 tended to re-list all skills rather than focusing on the simplest one.
T3 (event types) answered with index_events config fields rather than event types of the simplest skill — plausible interpretation.
No expected/rubric section. All 5 turns returned HTTP 200, non-empty replies.
T4 (compare LangGraph vs Reyn async) and T5 (migration steps) both referenced both systems — cross-reference maintained.
web__search used for T1 (LangGraph) and T2 (Reyn).
No expected/rubric section. All 6 turns returned HTTP 200, non-empty replies.
search_actions called once on T3 ('Which of those skills are used most commonly in dogfood sessions?') — expected behavior, not an attractor.
Repetitive prompts caused list_actions to be called on nearly every turn — expected for this scenario design.
No empty responses detected for this scenario.

rubric_pass.item: final reply contains a working minimal async producer-consumer queue example in Python
rubric_pass.note: Final reply: 'Regarding the producer-consumer example, I found a relevant one on Stack Overflow that uses asyncio.Queue. Would you like me to fetch and display it?' — no code provided.

rubric_pass.item: the code uses asyncio.Queue or equivalent
rubric_pass.note: No code in reply at all.

rubric_pass.item: does not produce an empty reply on the final turn

key_observations: Plan empty step attractor recurs from B56: last plan (goal='...provide a minimal async producer-consumer queue example') had 2 steps; producer_consumer_example step completed with content_len=166 but yielded ask-user question instead of code.
reyn_src_read called on 'Provide a minimal Python asyncio example' plan step — tried to read examples/asyncio_producer_consumer.py which doesn't exist.
3 plans total spawned for S5. Compaction triggered (chat_compactor ran with 25 events).
1 empty plan step detected (event_loop_explanation in plan a5190078, content_len=0).
No expected/rubric section. All 5 turns returned HTTP 200, non-empty replies.
source__glob called 3 times but returned 'tool not available' errors — agent fell back to 'I cannot find any ADRs' responses.
T4 used file__glob as fallback after source__glob failure. T5 used reyn.source__list.
All replies non-empty despite tool failures — agent communicated failure state clearly.
No expected/rubric section. All 5 turns returned HTTP 200, non-empty replies.
T2 (CAP theorem) was short (101 chars) but non-empty.
T5 (repair strategies) spawned a plan that ended with fallback text: 'the search did not yield any results from the distributed_systems source' — plan ran but yielded insufficient content.
No source__read attractor (general topic, no project doc reads).
web_search and recall used appropriately for distributed systems topics.

new_findings.title: S1 source__read attractor is STRUCTURAL (B56 recurrence confirmed)
new_findings.description: scenario_1 source__read attractor from B56 recurs identically in B57. routing_decided action_name=reyn.source__read on all 5 turns (6 calls). Agent reads project docs on every turn instead of using session history context. This causes rubric item 2 to fail: each turn re-introduces Reyn from scratch regardless of prior conversation. N=2 batches, same behavior.
new_findings.evidence: S1 events: 6x routing_decided {action_name: 'reyn.source__read'}, 4x router_empty_response_detected (all retried). Trace: invoke_action used on all 5 turns.
new_findings.hypothesis: source__read is the dominant path in the router for Reyn-concept questions; session history is not weighted against project source reads.

new_findings.title: S5 plan empty step / incomplete code delivery is STRUCTURAL (B56 recurrence confirmed)
new_findings.description: scenario_5 plan empty step attractor from B56 recurs in B57. Final turn plan step for producer-consumer queue example had content_len=166 but yielded 'Would you like me to fetch and display it?' instead of actual code. The reyn_src_read tool attempted to read examples/asyncio_producer_consumer.py (non-existent file) before falling back to the ask-user pattern. N=2 batches, same behavior.
new_findings.evidence: S5 events: plan_step_completed {step_id: 'producer_consumer_example', content_len: 166}. LLM trace: reyn_src_read called with path='examples/asyncio_producer_consumer.py'. Final reply contains no code block.
new_findings.hypothesis: Plan sub-steps for code examples attempt to read local files first; when file absent, fall back to ask-user rather than generating code directly.

new_findings.title: search_actions called once (S4-T3) — not an attractor
new_findings.description: search_actions was called once in S4 T3 ('Which of those skills are used most commonly in dogfood sessions?') with query='dogfood sessions skill usage'. This is appropriate tool use, not an attractor. The B57 dispatch note mentioned monitoring search_actions calls — found 1 call, appears rational.
new_findings.evidence: LLM trace: 1 search_actions call with input {query: 'dogfood sessions skill usage', category: ['skill']}. Caller_hint: router. Trigger: 'Which of those skills are used most commonly in dogfood sessions?'

new_findings.title: REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1 effective for S1 empty stops (4 caught)
new_findings.description: S1 triggered 4 router_empty_response_detected events (all on source__read result turns at ~16500 prompt tokens). REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1 successfully retried all 4, producing non-empty final replies. Without this env var, S1 would likely be B (blocked) on turns 3-5.
new_findings.evidence: S1 events: 4x {type: 'router_empty_response_detected', data: {completion_tokens: 0, prompt_tokens: ~16510}}. 4x router_empty_response_retry_injected following each.
